task.name: operation_span_rdoc
paradigm_classes: ordered sequence reproduction via keyboard navigation

stimuli.description: Processing task: an 8x8 black/white pattern (distractor grid) requiring a binary symmetric/not-symmetric keyboard judgment.

Reproduction phase: the 4x4 recall grid is interactive (arrow-key cursor shown via .response-active/.first-active). Participant navigates and confirms the remembered cell locations in presentation order.

response.correct_sequence_js: (() => { try { const rows = jsPsych.data.get().values(); const posOf = r => (r.spatial_location ?? r.square ?? r.position ?? r.location ?? r.stim_index); let lastRecall = -1; for (let i=0;i<rows.length;i++){ if(/recall|reproduc|response-grid/i.test(String(rows[i].trial_id||rows[i].trial_type||''))) lastRecall=i; } const block = rows.slice(lastRecall+1); const seq = block.filter(r => posOf(r)!==undefined && typeof posOf(r)!=='object').map(posOf); return seq.length ? seq : null; } catch(e){ return null; } })()

steps.description: Advance jsPsych instructions plugin pages (default key_forward = ArrowRight).

timing.trial_context_js: (() => { try { const c = document.querySelector('.cue-text, .prompt_box_simple, .prompt_box_operation, .prompt_box_response'); return c ? c.innerText.trim().slice(0,120) : ''; } catch(e){ return ''; } })()

notes.recall_mechanic: Keyboard-navigated reproduction: cursor moved with arrow keys across the 4x4 recall grid (.container > .box), each target confirmed with the select key (spacebar per style.css: .spacebar-box). No per-target clickable elements, so response_elements is intentionally omitted; the ordered targets are exposed via correct_sequence_js.
notes.cdp_dwell_derivation: No per-trial response window is readable from the (heavily truncated) experiment.js source, so cdp_dwell_ms falls back to the documented 200ms default.
notes.source_limitations: experiment.js is truncated after the OG_CHEIN_SYMM_GRIDS definitions; timeline construction, exact response-key handlers, and recorded data field names are not visible. response_key_js uses the multi-source window.correctResponse chain (Pattern A) to avoid guessing keys; correct_sequence_js reconstructs the target order defensively from recorded rows and returns null on error. Presentation-phase field names (spatial_location/square/position/location/stim_index) should be verified against a pilot export.
notes.set_size: Operation/symmetry span uses variable set sizes per block; whether set size is adaptive is not determinable from the available source.

reasoning_chain.inference: Identified paradigm 'operation_span_rdoc' with 2 stimuli.
